Angel Babies
(559) 248-8579
Angel Babies of Hinds Hospice
Angel Babies offers a perinatal program designed for families whose unborn child has been diagnosed with a terminal condition, babies who need special care, and families who have experienced miscarriage, stillbirth, or other infant death.
Save
Anthem Blue Cross
1 (800) 227-3238
Anthem Medi-Cal
Anthem health plan serves Medi-Cal families and individuals. Medi-Cal is now available to all people 50 years of age or older who meet eligibility criteria. Immigration status does not matter.
View Anthem Benefits.
Save
Aria Community Health Center (FQHC)
1 (559) 386-4500
Riverdale and Coalinga
www.ariachc.org
Save
Benefits Cal
1 (855) 832-8082
(Local DSS Center)
benefitscal.com
BenefitsCal.com is the new website where Fresno County residents are able to apply for, or manage their CalFresh, CalWORKS, General Relief and/or Medi-Cal benefits.
Save
CalSavers Independent Retirement
(855) 650-6918
www.calsavers.com
CalSavers is California’s retirement savings program for workers who do not have a way to save for retirement Register through your employer or sign up yourself.
Save
CalViva Health
1 (877) 618-0903
(Enroll)
1 (888) 893-1569
(Member Services)
www.calvivahealth.org
CalViva Health is a local care plan under the Medi-Cal Managed Care Program. Medi-Cal is now available to all people 50 years of age or older who meet eligibility criteria. Immigration status does not matter.
View CalViva Benefits
Save
California Alternative Rates for Energy (CARE)
1 (866) 743-2273
CARE Program
The California Alternate Rates for Energy (CARE) program is a monthly discount of 20% or more on gas and electricity. Participants qualify by meeting income guidelines or enrolling in certain public assistance programs.
Save
Catholic Charities
(559) 237-0851
ccdof.org
Catholic Charities is thought of as the first responders in helping neighbors with food, clothing, and other necessities. They also provide access to diapers, referral for families seeking shelter, Cal Fresh and Medi-Cal enrollment, California DMV ID Voucher, immigrant services, disaster relief, and more.
Save
Central CA Food Bank
(559) 237-3663
ccfoodbank.org
Central California Food Bank is the largest food bank in Central California serving Fresno, Madera, Kings, Kern and Tulare Counties. Programs include food distributions, Groceries2Go, School Lunch, First Fruits Market, CalFresh Outreach, and more.
Save
Centro La Familia Advocacy
(559) 237-2961
centrolafamilia.org
CLFA offers a range of programs to assist victims, families, immigrants, promote health and wellness, encourage civic engagement, and more. Services available in English, Spanish, and Hmong.
Save
1 (833) 678-2781
www.clinicasierravista.org
North Fine Community Health Center
Regional Medical Community Health Center
Elm Women’s and Pediatric Community Health Center
Save
Clinica Sierra Vista Behavioral Health
(833) 678-2781
www.clinicasierravista.org
Behavioral health services available to youth and adults. Services may include counseling, rehabilitation, recovery, psychiatric, medication management, and more.
Save
Covered California
1 (877) 752-4737
www.coveredca.com
Covered California is a free service that connects Californians with brand-name health insurance providers. Covered California can assist citizens and legal residents with applying for marketplace coverage.
Save
DBH Access Line (non-emergency)
1 (800) 654-3937
Fresno County DBH
Anyone may call the 24/7 Access line for information on Mental Health and Substance Use services, screenings, referrals, and linkages and/or eligibility information for various services.
Save
Economic Opportunities Commission Head Start
(559) 263-1527
fresnoeoc.org/headstart
Offers a no-cost comprehensive educational, emotional, social, health and nutritional services to preschool aged children (0-5 years) and their families. Special needs children welcomed!
Save
Exceptional Parents Unlimited (EPU)
(559) 229-2000
www.exceptionalparents.com
EPU is an Early Learning and Intervention service for children with special development needs and at-risk children, offers family resources, parent trainings, and child advocacy.
Save
Exodus Recovery
(559) 453-1008
exodusrecovery.com
A recovery focused environment where youth and adults in crisis can be assessed for stabilization services, medication evaluation and management, or hospitalization if necessary. 24 Hour / Walk-In Program.
Save
(866) 583-5190
https://www.fhcn.org
Fresno ACC | 290 N Wayte Ln, Fresno, CA 93701
Save
Fresno County Department of Social Services
(559) 600-6400
Fresno County DSS
Department of Social Services works to help adults, children, and families. They provide assistance to many programs such as: Medi-Cal, Cal-Fresh, CalWorks, Foster Care, Veterans, and more!
Save
Fresno County Public Library
(559) 600-7323
www.fresnolibrary.org
Fresno County Public Library provides collections and services through its Central Resource Library and 34 branches. Kids, teens, adults, and seniors can all find something whether it’s a book, arts and crafts, games, or special event. Check out the Events Calendar weekly!
Save
Fresno Family Counseling
(559) 795-5990
www.fresnofamilytherapy.com
Fresno Family Counseling offers evidence based and professional counseling to individuals, couples, children, and families. Professionals can help reduce anxiety, depression, unwanted habits, relationship struggles, lack of confidence, and other concerns. CalViva/Anthem Medi-Cal accepted.
Save
Fresno Housing
(559) 443-8400
Fresno Housing EHV Program
The Emergency Housing Voucher (EHV) program is made available to help assist individuals and families who are: homeless, fleeing, or have a high risk of housing instability.
Save
Fresno Mission/City Center
www.fresnomission.org/rescue
www.citycenterfresno.org/get-help
(559) 269-0839
The Fresno Mission and City Center are open to anyone in the community in need of help. They offer 24-hour emergency services, food services, chapel services, recovery programs, family centers, and more.
Save
Fresno PARCs Programs
(559) 621-2489
www.fresno.gov/parks
PARCS Department offers acres of open green spaces and a team devoted to providing organized recreation programs and activities for the community. Children and families can enjoy after school programs, family nights, camps, and more!
Save
Fresno State Connect Initiative
(855) 456-9995
FSU Connect Initiative
The Fresno State Connect Initiative provides low-cost internet services outreach. Programs are available for internet services as low as $10/month to eligible households. Some households are also eligible for a free Chromebook laptop. Community members are encouraged to call for more information.
Save
www.gfho.org
Kings Winery Medical Clinic |
(559) 255-6476
North Mark Medical Clinic |
(559) 860-4925
Cedar University Clinic |
(559) 860-4900
Save
Help Me Grow
1 (866) 543-7464
www.helpmegrow.fcoe.org
Help Me Grow Fresno County can answer your questions about your child's development from birth to age five. Services are free and confidential. Trained Call Line Care Coordinators will listen and follow your family's questions.
Save
Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
(559) 263-1320
fresnoeoc.org/liheap
LIHEAP provides a one-time financial assistance for energy bills in Fresno County per calendar year. If you are an eligible household with electricity, gas, propane, wood, or oil and need financial assistance, apply now.
Save
Marjaree Mason Center
(559) 233-HELP
(4357)
mmcenter.org
MMC provides a 24-hr hotline, emergency safe housing, transitional safe housing, victim advocacy, support groups, legal services, individual and group counseling, case management, domestic violence education, a batterer’s intervention program, anger management classes. Services are confidential and free.
Save
Maternal Mental Health Hotline
1 (833) 852-6262
NMMHH
The National Maternal Mental Health Hotline provides 24/7, free, confidential support before, during, and after pregnancy. Callers can call or text professional counselors in real time or receive referrals to local support providers. Interpreter services in 60 languages.
Save
Medically Indigent Services Program (MISP)
1 (559) 600-6580
Fresno County MISP
The Fresno County Medically Indigent Services Program (MISP) assists qualified, low-income Fresno County residents who have a medical need, but have no source of health coverage available and no other way to pay for necessary medical care.
Save
National Suicide and Crisis Lifeline: 988
988
988lifeline.org
Call, text, or chat for yourself or if worried about a loved one who may need crisis support. 24/7 access to trained crisis counselors who can help people experiencing mental health-related distress.
Save
(866) 707-6664
omnifamilyhealth.org
Omni Family Health Reedley
Omni Family Health Women’s Health Fresno
Save
Perinatal Wellness Center
(559) 600-1033
The Perinatal Wellness Center provides mental health services to pregnant and postpartum mothers and their babies, as well as to fathers struggling with paternal postnatal depression through in-home or office setting.
Save
Planned Parenthood
1 (877) 855-7526
Planned Parenthood Mar Monte
Planned Parenthood delivers vital reproductive health care, sex education, and information to women, men, teens, & transgender community. With or without insurance, you can receive care.
Save
Poverello House
(559) 498-6988
poverellohouse.org
Provides hot meals, clothing, a residential rehabilitation program, food bag distribution, showers, laundry, and a daytime shelter.
Save
Pregnancy Care Center
(559) 237-0683
www.pregnancycarecenter.com
Pregnancy Care Center is a medical center that offers a limited scope of pregnancy-related services. Services include pregnancy tests, ultrasound, education, classes, and referrals. All services are free and confidential.
Save
Reading and Beyond
(559) 342-8600
www.readingandbeyond.org
Services to help improve child’s reading level and comprehension, after-school tutoring, preschool, parent workshops, and more.
Save
Adventist Health
Adventist Health Medical Office Kerman |
(559) 846-9370
Adventist Health Dental Kingsburg |
(559) 897-6610
Adventist Health Specialty Care Selma |
(559) 856-6110
Medical Office: Adventist Health Fowler |
(559) 834-1614
Women’s Health: Adventist Health Reedley |
(559) 391-3115
Save
Sierra Meadows - Birdie Lou Counseling
(559) 900-7110
Sierra Meadows Foundation
Provides free therapy services to those in Fresno County experiencing mild to moderate mental health symptoms. Services offered are individual therapy for adults and children, couples, families, and a variety of groups to address a wide range of mental healthcare concerns.
Save
Smile California Medi-Cal Dental Program
1 (800) 322-6384
smilecalifornia.org
Smile, California is the new campaign to help Medi-Cal members make the most of their Medi-Cal dental benefits. Members can review offered benefits, watch educational videos, find a dentist, and more.
Save
Supportive Services Inc
(559) 230-2030
www.ssifresno.org
Supportive Services, Inc. Alternative Payment Program is a parental choice childcare subsidy program. The program may pay for all or part of the cost of childcare for children ages birth through 12 years (children with exceptional needs may be served to age 21). Must apply to get on Waiting List.
Save
USA Gov Financial Hardships
1 (844) 872-4681
www.usa.gov/financial-hardship
Learn how to apply for government programs to help with food, bills, housing, and more.
Save
1 (800) 492-4227
https://www.unitedhealthcenters.org
UHC Fresno Blackstone Health Center
UHC Fresno Bullard Health Center
UHC Fresno Milburn Health Center
UHC Fresno Minnewawa Health Center
UHC Fresno Tuolumne Health Center
UHC Fowler Health Center
UCH Huron Health Center
UHC Kerman Health Center
UHC Mendota Health Center
UHC Orange Grove Health Center
UHC Parlier Manning Health Center
UHC Reedley Health Center
UHC Selma Health Center- Highland Ave
UHC Selma Health Center – Rose Ave
Save
United Way - Free Tax Prep
2-1-1
www.uwfm.org/freetaxprep
United Way Fresno provides free tax preparations to low- and moderate-income individuals. Tax returns are prepared by trained and IRS-certified volunteers at local community organizations. Make your Free Tax Prep appointment by calling 211 or texting TAXES to 211-211.
Save
(559) 693-2462
San Joaquin Health Center
Central Fresno Community Health Center
Clovis Community Health Center (Urgent Care Available)
Kerman Health Center
Kerman Unified School District Community Health Center (open to public)
Save
Women, Infant, and Children (WIC)
1 (800) 852-5770
California WIC Program
WIC helps families by providing nutrition education, breastfeeding support, healthy foods, and referrals to health care and other community services. WIC serves children 0 to 5 and people who are pregnant or have given birth or experienced pregnancy loss.
No resources available.
